title: avenula pratensis subsp. vasconica
intro: Avenula pratensis subsp. vasconica, also known as the Vasconian meadow-grass, is a subspecies of grass native to the Vasconia region in Spain and France. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Avenula pratensis subsp. vasconica include soothing digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers, as well as treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Avenula pratensis subsp. vasconica include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
